text.title: accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for accessibility. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every mouse action must have a keyboard equivalent. Tab and Shift-Tab must traverse all interactive elements in logical order. Esc must close modals and dropdowns.
rule.rationale: Screen reader users and motor-impaired users navigate via keyboard. Logical tab order prevents confusion; Escape is the universal "cancel" signal.

rule.statement: All text and interactive elements must meet WCAG AA contrast. Test in context (not in isolation). Never relay information with color alone.
rule.rationale: Color-blind users and low-vision users cannot distinguish color-only indicators. Testing in isolation misses real-world failures (text over gradients, secondary surfaces).

rule.statement: All form fields must have associated labels. Errors must be announced to screen readers via aria-invalid and aria-describedby. Focus must move to error summary on submission failure.
rule.rationale: Screen reader users cannot infer field purpose from placeholder text. Error announcements prevent silent failures. Focus management ensures users see the error without manual navigation.
